prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a completely black frame.

It then cuts to a bathroom scene. A shirtless adult man stands facing the camera in the center of the frame. He is wearing a gray towel wrapped around his waist. The bathroom has pale tiled walls, a sink and mirror on the left, a shower head and beige shower curtain behind him, and towels hanging on both sides. The man’s posture is upright and direct, with his gaze oriented toward the viewer/camera.

By the final sampled frame, the setting has changed to an outdoor beach. The same man appears shirtless, now wearing white pants and sitting on a white horse. He holds up a red product container in one hand while looking toward the camera. A striped garment or towel is draped over one shoulder. The ocean and sandy beach are visible in the background, with part of a palm tree at the upper right. On-screen text appears in the sky area: “SMELL LIKE A MAN, MAN.” with “Old Spice” written below it in a script-style logo.
